title: podman info --debug fails under windows when running inside ubuntu:18.04 container
body:
````
holst@0c46f2b623a6:~/tugboat$ podman version
Version:            1.5.1
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.4
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
holst@0c46f2b623a6:~/tugboat$ podman info --debug
WARN[0000] Error initializing configured OCI runtime crun: no valid executable found for OCI runtime crun: invalid argument
cannot clone: Operation not permitted
Error: could not get runtime: cannot re-exec process
WARN[0000] unable to find /home/holst/.config/containers/registries.conf. some podman (image shortnames) commands may be limited
````

````
PS C:\Users\henri> docker version
Client: Docker Engine - Community
 Version:           19.03.2
 API version:       1.40
 Go version:        go1.12.8
 Git commit:        6a30dfc
 Built:             Thu Aug 29 05:26:49 2019
 OS/Arch:           windows/amd64
 Experimental:      false

Server: Docker Engine - Community
 Engine:
  Version:          19.03.2
  API version:      1.40 (minimum version 1.12)
  Go version:       go1.12.8
  Git commit:       6a30dfc
  Built:            Thu Aug 29 05:32:21 2019
  OS/Arch:          linux/amd64
  Experimental:     false
 containerd:
  Version:          v1.2.6
  GitCommit:        894b81a4b802e4eb2a91d1ce216b8817763c29fb

  GitCommit:        fec3683
PS C:\Users\henri>
````

comments.body: @hholst80 do you have `/usr/bin/runc` installed?  If so you can you try `podman --runtime /usr/bin/runc`

````
holst@0c46f2b623a6:~$ podman --runtime /usr/sbin/runc
WARN[0000] Error initializing configured OCI runtime crun: no valid executable found for OCI runtime crun: invalid argument
cannot clone: Operation not permitted
Error: could not get runtime: cannot re-exec process
WARN[0000] unable to find /home/holst/.config/containers/registries.conf. some podman (image shortnames) commands may be limited
holst@0c46f2b623a6:~$ type runc
runc is /usr/sbin/runc
holst@0c46f2b623a6:~$
holst@0c46f2b623a6:~$ /usr/sbin/runc --version
runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev
holst@0c46f2b623a6:~$
````
Under windows meaning? Are you attempting to run Podman inside a container being run by Docker?
What is the OS of the container fedora 31?

it appears you are not running the podman remote client binary.  this is the only supported binary on windows and like docker, connects to a linux vm to run.  when running the podman remote client binary on windows, it will report an API version which is absent in your output.  moreover, this explains the oci runtime problems.

im not sure which instructions you used for your setup, but in the case you are building this from master or a source tree, you can build the remote client binary with `make podman-remote-windows`
@baude I think he may be running Podman in a Docker container running Ubuntu on Windows. Without knowing more about the setup, though, I don't think we can reasonably comment on debugging.

Docker Engine hosted on Windows.

The container is ubuntu:18.04. The OS/kernel I am not sure, in the old days it was boot2docker but now its something proprietary https://www.docker.com/products/docker-desktop 
It's probably not a big secret, I just cannot find the architectural design of it right now.

Explicit repro steps:

1. Obtain a machine with Windows 10.
2. Install Docker Desktop for Windows https://www.docker.com/products/docker-desktop
3. Start a container from the command prompt: `docker run -it --rm ubuntu:18.04`
4. Install podman inside the container. Follow this guide: https://computingforgeeks.com/how-to-install-podman-on-ubuntu/
5. Still inside the container, run: `podman info --debug`
@hholst80, thanks for sharing. This environment is quite exotic but I don't want to give up yet. Can you share the output when running `podman info --debug`?
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.

Podman will not work in a locked down container yet.  We can get a lot further by turning off some security features.

RUn a --privileged container with Docker and  podman should be able to function,
@giuseppe, can you have a look at it? It looks like `crun` is cloning which would be surprising for the `info` use case where we only need the version and commit.

> ```
> holst@0c46f2b623a6:~/tugboat$ podman info --debug
> WARN[0000] Error initializing configured OCI runtime crun: no valid executable found for OCI runtime crun: invalid argument
> cannot clone: Operation not permitted
> Error: could not get runtime: cannot re-exec process
> WARN[0000] unable to find /home/holst/.config/containers/registries.conf. some podman (image shortnames) commands may be limited
> ```

Just had a look at the code, it has nothing to do with `crun`. The cloning error comes from Podman trying to create a new user namespace for running rootless.

@hholst80 are you running the container as `--privileged`?
Clone failures usually are related to seccomp blocking them.  Can you try disabling it?

> Just had a look at the code, it has nothing to do with `crun`. The cloning error comes from Podman trying to create a new user namespace for running rootless.
> 
> @hholst80 are you running the container as `--privileged`?

No, not running as privileged.
@hholst80 can you try running the container (in which you're running Podman) as privileged?

Closing. Please reopen in case it's not working in a privileged container.

The support for unprivileged containers will improve with https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4698.
